None of these symbols exist in the codebase. The plan asserts 'status: Accepted — it documents DECISIONS that are IMPLEMENTED on this epic's stack,' but the cited implementations are absent — making the ADR's verifiability claim a capability assertion that cannot currently be verified.

findings.finding: The local session keyspace (`session:<id>` / `code-review: session:{id}`) and its isolation from the Gerrit change keyspace are documented as implemented, but no session-keyed artifact creation or `latest_code_review_result` title-scheme matcher exists — only the Gerrit keyspace (`code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}` in `voter.py:197`) is verifiably implemented.
findings.location: What section — bullet 5: 'Typed session/change keyspaces' / AC1
findings.suggested_fix: In the ADR, scope bullet 5 as: 'In v1 only the Gerrit keyspace is active (`code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}`); the local session keyspace (`code-review: session:{id}`) is defined but not yet emitted — so no local review can pollute a Gerrit first-review prior because no local artifact exists.'

The actual registrations are `code_review_base_output`, `code_review_findings`, `code_review_coach`, and `code_review_verification` — no `code_review_novelty`.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: Destructive/irreversible operation(s) with no stated safeguard.
findings.suggested_fix: State the safeguard (backup, dry-run, reversible migration, rollback) or justify the irreversibility explicitly (T4).

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the MECHANISM by which `latest_code_review_result` enforces keyspace disjointness: either (a) define the title-scheme field that is stored in the sidecar payload at emit time and filtered at read time, and add it to the `build_payload` spec; or (b) document that disjointness is ticket-level (local sessions write to a session-scoped ticket ID that can never be the same as a Gerrit change's `target_ticket`) and explain how that structural separation is guaranteed.
findings.finding: The keyspace disjointness guarantee ('local session must NOT seed a change's first Gerrit review') is described as a structural property enforced by the reader matching 'only its own title scheme,' but the isolation mechanism is not structural at the storage layer — both local and Gerrit code-review sidecars write `REVIEW_RESULT` events using the same event type on a `target_ticket`. If the same ticket is used as `target_ticket` for a local session and a Gerrit change, a reader that does not filter on a title-scheme would mix them. The disjointness claim depends entirely on filtering logic inside the unimplemented `latest_code_review_result` reader. The ADR will describe this as a structural guarantee, but it is actually a reader-implementation guarantee — and the failure mode (a local code-review prior seeding a Gerrit first review) directly contradicts the stated design intent.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section — 'Typed session/change keyspaces' bullet: 'the reader (latest_code_review_result) matches only its own title scheme, so the keyspaces are disjoint'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Typed session/change keyspaces' bullet and in AC3 of the ADR spec, add: 'The sidecar payload stores a `review_source` field (`session:<id>` vs `change:<id>@<revision>`) at emit time; `latest_code_review_result` filters on this field so only same-source priors are returned. Alternatively, if disjointness is enforced at the ticket-ID level (local → session ticket, Gerrit → change ticket, never the same ID), document this structural guarantee and its enforcement point.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'carryover labeling' in the plan body (What section) — explain what gets labeled, when, and which module implements it — so the review-kernel.md author has the same clarity as for the other three documented behaviors (region-gated drop, keying, resilience).
findings.finding: 'Carryover labeling' is used in the What section and AC4 as a required documentation topic for `docs/review-kernel.md`, but nowhere in the plan body is the term defined or described — an implementer has no guidance on what 'carryover labeling' means or what behavior it refers to.
findings.location: What section (last paragraph) and AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet in the What section such as: '**Carryover labeling** — when a prior finding is recognized as known (novelty score below floor and region-unchanged), the code-review result annotates it with a `carryover` label in the surfaced output (implemented in `<module>`), so downstream consumers can distinguish new findings from recurrent suppressed ones.' Adjust the module reference to the actual implementation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `verify.novelty_priority_floor` (the escape hatch for high-impact findings) to AC4's required content list for `docs/review-kernel.md`, so the consumer-facing doc captures both the convergence mechanism and its configurable override.
findings.finding: AC4 requires review-kernel.md to document 'carryover labeling, region-gated drop, keying, resilience' but omits the `verify.novelty_priority_floor` escape hatch — the key configurable threshold that prevents permanent suppression of high-impact findings. The 'What' section describes it as the escape hatch, but the AC does not bind the doc author to include it. A reader of review-kernel.md would learn the convergence mechanism but not how to tune or override it.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 (`docs/review-kernel.md` Code-review consumer section)
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC4 to: '`docs/review-kernel.md` (Code-review consumer section) documents the novelty convergence behavior (carryover labeling, region-gated drop, keying, resilience, and the `verify.novelty_priority_floor` escape hatch for high-impact findings).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `_hash_file` reuse boundary: either promote the function to a shared public utility (e.g., `review_kernel` or a `rebar.llm.fs_tools` helper) or explicitly document in the ADR why the internal cross-package import of a private symbol is the intended seam.
findings.finding: The plan proposes `code_review/sidecar.py::reviewed_file_hashes` reuse `plan_review.attest._hash_file` (a private, underscore-prefixed module-internal function) across package boundaries. Calling a private symbol from a sibling package creates an undocumented coupling that the plan does not address — the function should either be promoted to a shared utility (e.g., `rebar.llm.review_kernel` or `rebar.signing`) or the deliberate internal import should be explicitly acknowledged and justified in the ADR.
findings.location: What section — 'Content-addressed region detection' bullet: 'reusing `plan_review.attest._hash_file`, computed in `code_review/sidecar.py::reviewed_file_hashes`'
findings.suggested_fix: Promote `_hash_file` + `_ABSENT_HASH` to `rebar.llm.fs_tools` (which already exists at `src/rebar/llm/fs_tools.py`) as a public `hash_file(path, *, base) -> str` helper, then update both `plan_review/attest.py` and `code_review/sidecar.py` to import from that shared location. Document this promotion in the ADR's 'Reuse the novelty sub-call' bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the ADR a brief alternatives-considered note for the keyspace isolation mechanism (title-scheme matching vs. explicit namespace field vs. separate event types), explaining why title-scheme matching was chosen and what prevents false collisions across contexts.
findings.finding: The typed session/change keyspace isolation mechanism (disjoint `session:<id>` vs `change:<id>` keyspaces enforced purely by title-scheme matching in `latest_code_review_result`) is a novel data-isolation pattern. The plan does not show that alternative isolation approaches (separate event types, an explicit namespace field in the sidecar payload, a key-prefix registry) were considered before committing to title-scheme matching as the sole isolation mechanism.
findings.location: What section — 'Typed session/change keyspaces, and why local must NOT seed a change's first Gerrit review' bullet

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
